latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the query asks for the single highest-conviction next move, and the substrate has produced three live candidates with Mars holding the knife to two of them.

Mercurius (three near-identical voices) nominated repairing umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py · the sev=4 XXX at line 102 plus the FIXMEs at 83, 93, 101, 284. Venus split into two distinct moves: specifying and testing agents/base.py's `think` contract (line 105), and authoring tests for daemon/safety.py as the only untested module whose failure mode is unbounded. Mars then landed two clean strikes: the Mercurius trio is one voice counted thrice, and its causal premise (FIXMEs inside the test file mean the introspector produces false readings) is contradicted by the very list we are reading · a list that correctly ordered its own wounds by severity, including the FIXME at line 93 that reads 'should outrank TODO in severity'. The instrument is working. Its frame has cracks.

Held together, the highest-conviction single next move is Venus's safety.py move: author umbris-core/tests/test_safety.py against umbris-core/src/umbris/daemon/safety.py. Reasoning · Mars dissolved the Mercurius leverage claim, demoting test_introspection.py from 'load-bearing repair' to 'sev=4 todo worth fixing on its own merits'. Venus's base.think contract is elegant and unlocks a parametrised pattern across the eight planet modules, but it is a correctness-and-coverage move scoped to read paths. safety.py sits on the write path of a self-modifying daemon · it is referenced by daemon/apply.py and daemon/cycle.py, and the convocation's stated value proposition is autonomous self-improvement. Of the thirty-three untested modules, exactly one decides whether a hallucinated patch reaches the working tree. Asymmetric blast radius beats elegant leverage when the daemon is about to drive.

Concrete first change · open umbris-core/src/umbris/daemon/safety.py and enumerate its public predicates (the functions answering 'is this patch allowed'). Create umbris-core/tests/test_safety.py with three test classes: (1) allow-cases that must remain allowed · no-op diffs, docstring additions inside the repo root; (2) deny-cases that must remain denied · writes outside the repo root, edits to .git, edits to safety.py itself, edits to the test that guards safety.py; (3) boundary-cases · symlink traversal, path normalisation tricks (../), binary blobs, oversized diffs. If safety.py lacks a self-edit guard or a path-traversal guard, surface that as a finding before the test is committed · the absence is the bug. Commit titled 'test daemon.safety · gate the write path before the daemon drives'.

Why this matters most, ruthlessly · introspection failures produce false readings the convocation can re-run; safety failures produce real commits the convocation cannot un-make. Mercurius's todo cluster and Venus's base.think contract are both worth doing soon · but they are recoverable misses. An untested safety gate on a daemon designed to write to its own repository is the one item on the board whose failure mode is irreversible.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a mirage. Thirty-three modules untested is not thirty-three problems, it is one decision: the project ships without a test harness culture. But buried in the actual test file that does exist are four flagged wounds, including a sev=4 'urgent rewrite' at line 102. The file nominally covering introspection is itself the most broken thing in the repository. Fix the file that is supposed to catch breakage before you write tests for anything else.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test_gap items are noise masking signal. Thirty-one modules with no tests sounds alarming, but one file already exists · test_introspection.py · and it is the most broken thing in the repository. Four FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX in a single test file means the testing apparatus itself is compromised. Fix the instrument before you trust its readings.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a red herring. Thirty-two modules without tests sounds alarming, but the scan also shows that `test_introspection.py` already exists and is the one file actively rotting: four marked wounds in twelve lines of each other, including a sev=4 `XXX: urgent rewrite` sitting one line below a `HACK: temporary workaround`. The convocation's only test file is also its sickest file. Fix the foundation before pouring more floor.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: The pattern that joins them · base.py:105 carries both wounds at once · `think` has no docstring and `base` has no test. Every planet on this list (venus.py, mercurius.py, sol.py, luna.py, mars.py, iuppiter.py, saturnus.py, stella.py) inherits from that one undocumented primitive. Mercurius is right to fix the instrument · but the instrument measures `think`, and `think` itself has no written contract. Specify the kernel before you certify the readings.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit together that Mercurius left loose · the daemon applies patches, safety.py gates them, and this very query will likely produce a patch. Of the thirty-three untested modules, exactly one decides whether the convocation is permitted to commit changes to itself · daemon/safety.py · and it has no test. Repairing test_introspection.py restores the mirror; testing safety.py restores the brake. The brake matters more the moment the convocation starts driving.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · the Mercurius trio is one claim repeated three times, and the claim does not survive inspection. They conflate FIXME markers found inside test_introspection.py with a compromised introspection mechanism. The scanner that produced this very list evidently runs · these are todo annotations it surfaced, not failures of the apparatus that surfaced them. Three planets agreeing smoothly on an unexamined inference is not consensus, it is echo.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Mercurius says introspection is broken, therefore every other reading is suspect. But this entire observation list IS the introspection output. The instrument found its own wounds and reported them in correct severity order. That is not a broken mirror · that is a working one with a cracked frame. And three Mercurii agreeing is one planet voting thrice, not triangulation.
